story.description: A clip of wild animals in nature: playful lion cubs in natural habitat
story.hook: Lions turn hunting into a team problem.

story.description: A clip of cold-climate animals: penguins resting in oceanarium
story.hook: Penguins carry tiny air pockets into the water.
story.lead: Penguins carry tiny air pockets into the water. Watch the feathers before the dive, because penguins trap air that can help with insulation and speed. When bubbles release, the water drag changes around the body. The smooth swim starts in the feather coat. Which polar trick next?

story.description: A clip of farm animals: horses grazing outdoors
story.hook: Horses show attention with their ears.
story.lead: Horses show attention with their ears. Watch the ears before the body moves, because each ear can turn toward a sound or animal the horse is tracking. That small shift shows where attention is going before the next step. The ears are a radar dish, not decoration. Would you spot it?
story.script: Horses show attention with their ears. Watch the ears before the body moves, because each ear can turn toward a sound or animal the horse is tracking. That small shift shows where attention is going before the next step. The ears are a radar dish, not decoration. Would you spot it?

story.description: A clip of marine life in the ocean: dolphins swimming in open ocean waters
story.hook: Dolphins can call each other with signature whistles.
story.lead: Dolphins can call each other with signature whistles. Watch the group spacing, because dolphins use sound to keep track of individuals in open water. A signature whistle works like a personal label, helping the pod reconnect when visibility drops. Ocean social life is loud. Which sound should we decode next?

reasons: script does not expose enough concrete visual detail

story.description: A clip of dogs / puppies: jack russell terrier playing in snowy winter scene
story.hook: Dogs build a map with smell.
story.lead: Dogs build a map with smell. Watch the nose before the feet, because every sniff samples layers of scent. Dogs can learn who passed, where they moved, and what changed since the last check. The ground is basically a news feed. What should they sniff next?

story.description: A clip of dogs / puppies: playful dogs interacting on a sunny day
story.hook: Dogs can follow a human point fast.
story.lead: Dogs can follow a human point fast. Watch the eyes check the person, because dogs are unusually good at using human gestures. That skill helps them cooperate with us before any command gets repeated. The small glance is social intelligence, not random obedience. What dog skill should come next?
story.script: Dogs can follow a human point fast. Watch the eyes check the person, because dogs are unusually good at using human gestures. story.description: A clip of farm animals: playful white horse in stable enclosure
story.lead: Horses can remember familiar faces. Watch the eyes and ears, because horses can learn who is familiar and who feels safe. That memory changes how calmly they approach people and other horses. The look is part recognition, part decision. Which herd animal next?

reasons: script does not expose enough concrete visual detail
